summary:
Given two non-negative integers represented as strings, num1 and num2, return their product, also as a string.

You must compute the result without converting the inputs directly into a built-in big-integer type, so you implement the multiplication digit by digit, much like long multiplication on paper.
constraints: num1 and num2 consist of digits only
Neither input has leading zeros, except for the number 0 itself
Do not use a built-in BigInteger library or convert the strings to integers directly
